content: Hey, I'm thinking about going home for Thanksgiving this year. It's been a while since I've seen my parents.
That sounds nice! When was the last time you visited them?
I think it was last Christmas, actually. My mom keeps asking when I'm coming back. She's been really worried since I moved to San Diego for work.
How long have you been living in San Diego now?
About 8 months now. I started my new job at Nexus Labs back in March. It's been great, but I do miss my family sometimes.
What does your mom do for a living? Maybe you could tell her about your work at Nexus Labs to keep her updated.
She's a high school English teacher. My dad's a mechanic. They're both pretty traditional and don't really get what I do as a software engineer, but they're proud of me.
That's wonderful that they're supportive. Do you have any siblings?
Yeah, I have a younger sister named Emma. She's 19 and just started college this fall. She's studying art history, which is so different from what I do.
That's a big age gap between you two. Do you think she'll come home for Thanksgiving too?
Probably. She usually does, especially since she's just started college and might be homesick. Plus, our parents love having us all together.
It sounds like you have a close family. Do you all have any Thanksgiving traditions?
Oh yeah, we always have a big family dinner at our house. My mom makes her famous apple pie, and my dad always insists on carving the turkey even though he's terrible at it.
That's so sweet! It's great that you have those traditions. Are you planning to stay in San Diego long-term or think about moving back closer to your family eventually?
I'm not sure yet. I really like my job and my apartment here. I share it with two roommates - a guy named Mike who's also a software engineer, and a girl named Sarah who's in med school.
It sounds like you've got a good setup there. How do your roommates feel about you going home for the holidays?
They're cool with it. Mike usually goes to visit his family in LA, and Sarah's parents come to visit her here. So it works out well for all of us to have the place to ourselves during the holidays.
